title: 10 Herbal Creams For Motion Sickness
intro_desc: Herbal creams for motion sickness are gaining popularity as a natural remedy to help alleviate the discomfort of travel sickness. These creams often contain herbs that have been used for centuries to calm the mind and body. One example is ginger, also known as Zingiber officinale, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help ease nausea and dizziness. Valeriana officinalis, or valerian root, is another herb that's commonly used to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which are common symptoms of motion sickness. Passiflora incarnata, or passionflower, is also used to calm the nervous system and promote a sense of calm.  When we're feeling anxious or stressed, our body's natural response is to prepare for "fight or flight," which can exacerbate motion sickness. Herbal teas and creams, like those containing ginger, valerian root, and passionflower, can help calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. This can make a big difference in our lives, as motion sickness can be a major obstacle to travel and exploration. By using herbal creams or teas, we can feel more confident and prepared for our travels, and enjoy the experience without the added stress of motion sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting. Gingerol also has a drying effect on the stomach, which can help alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness. In addition, the creams contain zingerone, a compound that has anti-emetic properties, meaning it helps prevent vomiting. The warming and soothing effects of the creams also help to calm the stomach and reduce discomfort. The combination of these active constituents and properties makes Zingiber officinale creams a potential remedy for motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of ginger essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and stir in the vitamin E oil and ginger essential oil.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers or a silicone ice cube tray. Let it cool and harden completely.
Once hardened, label the containers and store them in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams contains valerenic acid and valepotriates, which are its active constituents. These compounds have sedative properties, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, a common cause of motion sickness. Valerenic acid also has antispasmodic properties, which can alleviate muscle tension and alleviate nausea associated with motion sickness. Additionally, valepotriates have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of motion sickness symptoms. By addressing the underlying causes of motion sickness, Valeriana officinalis creams can provide relief from nausea, dizziness, and other symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of dried Valeriana officinalis root, and 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add beeswax to the melted oil mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Add dried Valeriana officinalis root to the mixture and let it steep for 2 hours or overnight. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Mix the strained oil with jojoba oil and pour the mixture into a container. Let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata creams contains a bioactive constituent called flavonoids, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system. These flavonoids, specifically kaempferol and quercetin, help to reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to motion sickness. The cream also contains apigenin, a flavonoid that has a calming effect on the brain and can help to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata creams contain a compound called harmine, which has a sedative effect on the central nervous system and can help to reduce the symptoms of motion sickness. By reducing anxiety and stress, and promoting a sense of calm, Passiflora incarnata creams can help to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal creams for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Passiflora incarnata leaves. You can buy them online or from a herbal store.
Combine the dried leaves with 2 cups of water in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Add 1 cup of coconut oil or other carrier oil to the liquid. Let it cool.
Pour the cooled mixture into small containers. Label them and store them in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids as its active constituents. Flavonoids, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, help to improve blood flow to the inner ear and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to motion sickness. Terpenoids, such as ginkgolides and bilobalide, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to alleviate nausea and dizziness. These compounds may also help to improve cognitive function and reduce anxiety, which can exacerbate motion sickness. By addressing the underlying causes of motion sickness, Ginkgo biloba creams may provide relief from symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler over low heat, stirring until smooth.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and add the Ginkgo biloba extract. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a small bowl and let it cool until solid. This may take about 30 minutes.
Once solid, use a spoon to scoop out the Ginkgo biloba cream and store it in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains alkaloids like quinine, cinchonine, and cinchonidine, which are the active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, making them effective against motion sickness. The quinine in Cinchona officinalis creams helps to reduce nausea and dizziness by blocking the action of acetylcholine, a chemical that can stimulate the vomiting center in the brain. Cinchonidine also has a sedative effect, which can calm the stomach and prevent vomiting. By using Cinchona officinalis creams, you can get relief from motion sickness symptoms and stay comfortable during travel.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 2 cups of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Cinchona officinalis bark to the heated coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Let the mixture cool, then add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil. Stir until the beeswax is fully melted.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains linalool and linalyl acetate, two active constituents that help ease motion sickness. Linalool has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which calms the stomach and reduces nausea. Linalyl acetate, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, such as headaches and dizziness. The calming and anti-inflammatory properties of these constituents in Lavandula angustifolia creams can help reduce the body's response to motion, making it a potential solution for motion sickness. By reducing the physical and mental discomfort associated with motion sickness, these creams can help people feel more comfortable during travel.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 10 drops of Lavandula angustifolia essential oil
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water
Once melted, add beeswax and stir until it's fully incorporated and the mixture is smooth
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for a few minutes. Add 10 drops of Lavandula angustifolia essential oil and stir well
Pour the mixture into a tin or jar and let it cool completely before use. Apply a small amount to wrists or temples as needed for motion sickness relief

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams contains the active constituents avenanthramides and ferulic acid, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help to soothe the stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. Avenanthramides also have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to ease dizziness and disorientation caused by motion sickness. Ferulic acid has been shown to have a protective effect on the digestive system, helping to reduce the symptoms of motion sickness and prevent dehydration. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Avena sativa creams can help to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness and provide relief to those who suffer from it.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal creams for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler until the beeswax melts.
Add the dried Avena sativa to the melted mixture and heat for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool. Stir in the vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains active constituents like linalool and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to reduce muscle tension and relax the digestive system, making it easier to manage motion sickness symptoms. The calming effects of Melissa officinalis creams also help to reduce anxiety and stress, which can trigger motion sickness. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties can help to soothe stomach cramps and nausea associated with motion sickness. By reducing these symptoms, Melissa officinalis creams can provide relief from motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and a double boiler.
Combine the Melissa officinalis flowers and coconut oil in the double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours, or until the oil turns a light green color.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Pour the infused oil into a glass jar and store in a cool, dark place for up to 2 weeks before using in creams.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains boiactive constituents such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that contribute to motion sickness. The flavonoids, particularly luteolin and kaempferol, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help calm the nervous system. Glycyrrhizin also has a stabilizing effect on the digestive system, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. By reducing inflammation and stress, and stabilizing the digestive system, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may provide relief from motion sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups water, 1 cup glycerin, 1 cup vegetable oil, 1/4 cup beeswax, 2 tablespoons glycyrrhiza glabra powder, 2 tablespoons aloe vera gel.
Combine water, glycerin, and vegetable oil in a saucepan. Heat over low heat, stirring until beeswax melts.
Remove saucepan from heat and let cool for 5 minutes. Add glycyrrhiza glabra powder and aloe vera gel. Mix well.
Pour mixture into a separate container. Let cool and thicken for 30 minutes.
Once thickened, apply a small amount to a cotton pad or directly to skin as needed for motion sickness relief. Store in an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum creams contains piperine and other bioactive constituents, including borneol, camphene, and limonene. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce nausea and discomfort caused by motion sickness. The terpenes in piper nigrum creams, such as camphene and borneol, can help relax muscles and improve blood circulation, which may alleviate symptoms of motion sickness. Piper nigrum's antispasmodic properties, thanks to the presence of limonene, can also help soothe stomach cramps and reduce the sensation of nausea. The combination of these properties makes piper nigrum creams a potential remedy for motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Piper nigrum herbal creams for motion sickness
remedy_procedure: Mix 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of piper nigrum extract and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the saucepan.
Heat the mixture until the beeswax melts, then remove from heat.
Let the mixture cool and thicken for 10-15 minutes before whipping it with an electric mixer.
Pour the cream into a container and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent motion sickness. Ginger tea, in particular, is a popular choice. Ginger has natural properties that can calm your stomach and reduce nausea. Some people find that sipping on ginger tea before traveling or engaging in activities that trigger motion sickness helps minimize their symptoms.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for motion sickness every day can be safe, but moderation is key. Some teas, like ginger and peppermint, are generally considered safe for daily use. However, overdoing it can cause digestive issues like stomach upset and diarrhea. Listen to your body and adjust your consumption accordingly.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like ginger and peppermint can help with motion sickness. Some people notice relief within 30 minutes to an hour after drinking it. It's best to have the tea 30 minutes to an hour before traveling or getting on a boat. The results can last for several hours, but it may need to be taken again for continued relief.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for motion sickness can be most effective when you're feeling queasy. Try sipping it about 30 minutes before you travel, and then take small sips as needed. Some herbal teas, like ginger or peppermint, can help calm your stomach and ease nausea.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile can help with motion sickness, but be cautious if you're taking prescription medications for it. Some herbal teas might interact with your medication, making it stronger or weaker than it should be, which can be unsafe. It's best to talk to your doctor about any teas you drink.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some teas, like ginger tea, are sometimes used for motion sickness. However, not all herbal teas are safe during pregnancy. It's essential to research the specific tea you're considering and ensure it's not associated with any potential risks to your unborn baby.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas like ginger and peppermint can help with motion sickness. These teas are generally safe for breastfeeding mothers to consume in moderation. However, it's essential to check the tea's ingredients and avoid any that may have unknown effects on the baby. Some herbal teas can pass into breast milk, so it's crucial to be cautious.
title_2: Say Goodbye to Motion Sickness with 10 Herbal Creams for Boat Riders
